message:
fix(server): add atomic drain gate to ConnectionManager and deterministic pool acquisition

ConnectionManager had a race between begin_drain() on the shutdown signal
and register(): a new connection could sneak in between the drain signal
and the next register() call that checks is_shutting_down(). Fix by adding
an accepting AtomicBool gate that begin_drain() closes while holding the
connection state lock, guaranteeing no registration completes after drain
begins.

ConnectionManager is now Clone (all fields are Arc-wrapped) so the HTTP
listener can share it with external code that needs to trigger drain
independently of the shutdown signal.

New begin_drain() method on Http1Listener provides a cleaner API than
the previous shutdown_signal().begin_drain() pattern, using the listener's
configured drain timeout.

HTTP connection pool: try_acquire() previously used HashMap iteration order
for idle connection selection, which is randomized per-process. Now
explicitly selects the connection with the lowest ID via .min(), making
connection reuse deterministic and testable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
